novel_features.name: Top posts
novel_features.description: Rank recent posts by upvotes, comments, or engagement and return them with full content.
novel_features.rationale: The 'signal in the noise' query — Skool's UI shows newest-first; sorting by engagement inside a time window with full content is a per-call composition the live API can't do.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this for a daily/weekly cron that surfaces the 3-5 most-engaging posts from any community — perfect for catching up without scrolling.

novel_features.name: Leaderboard
novel_features.description: Top members by points for the community, with level and bio fields included.
novel_features.rationale: Skool's leaderboard is rendered into a single page response; we extract and project it into an agent-friendly shape with --select / --top.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs the current community leaderboard in one call without scraping the page.

novel_features.name: Digest since
novel_features.description: Aggregate everything new across posts, comments, members, and lessons since a timestamp.
novel_features.rationale: Time-windowed cross-entity aggregate; would otherwise require multiple API calls + glue.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs a single brief of community activity for a daily/weekly cron.

novel_features.name: Cross-community SQL
novel_features.description: Run read-only SQL across every community in your local store.
novel_features.rationale: Owns multi-community sync; no incumbent supports cross-community joins.
novel_features.example: skool-pp-cli sql 'SELECT community, COUNT(*) FROM posts GROUP BY community'
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs to compose a query across multiple Skool communities you own or operate.

novel_features.name: Calendar to ICS
novel_features.description: Export upcoming community events to an .ics file for Google Cal / Outlook.
novel_features.rationale: No OSS solution today. Requires synthesizing Skool's calendar data into the iCalendar format.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when a member wants community events on their personal calendar without manual entry.

novel_features.name: Classroom export
novel_features.description: Export an entire course to a markdown bundle (modules, lessons, attachments, video URLs).
novel_features.rationale: Requires recursive walk + Mux URL extraction + filesystem write; no incumbent ships a one-shot bundler.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs to ingest a course for offline reference, search, or LLM retrieval.

narrative.headline: Every Skool community feature, plus a local SQLite mirror, FTS, and cross-community ops no other Skool tool ships.
narrative.value_prop: Pulls every post, comment, member, course, lesson, and calendar event into a local SQLite store with FTS5 so you can query historical state, compute leaderboard deltas, and surface at-risk members the native UI cannot show. One auth_token cookie, two hosts (www.skool.com reads, api2.skool.com writes), zero CloudFront friction.
narrative.auth_narrative: Skool has no public API. Authenticate with the auth_token JWT cookie from your logged-in browser session: `skool-pp-cli auth set-token` (writes ~/.config/skool-pp-cli/config.toml). Same cookie covers reads and writes; CloudFront requires a realistic User-Agent which the CLI sets automatically.

quickstart.comment: Import auth_token cookie from Chrome (or write the token directly to ~/.config/skool-pp-cli/config.toml)
What's new in the community in the last 24h
Export a course to a markdown bundle

troubleshoots.fix: Run `skool-pp-cli doctor`; the buildId likely rotated. The CLI auto-refetches but you can force `skool-pp-cli buildid refresh`.
Your auth_token expired or User-Agent is missing. Run `skool-pp-cli auth status` then `auth set-token` with a fresh cookie.
Need at least two snapshots. Run `skool-pp-cli sync` over multiple days, or `sync --snapshot-now` to seed two points.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when you operate or moderate a Skool community and need analytics, exports, or automation that the native UI cannot do — at-risk member detection, leaderboard movement over time, classroom-to-markdown export, cross-community SQL, or scheduled digests for an agent.

recipes.title: Signal-in-noise: top 5 posts this week
recipes.explanation: Full content, sorted by engagement (upvotes + 3*comments). The headline command for noise-heavy communities you can't keep up with.

recipes.title: Daily community digest
recipes.explanation: Tight 24h roll-up. Field-selected so an agent can drop into a Slack/email digest with minimal context.

recipes.title: Top 10 members snapshot
recipes.explanation: Current 30d leaderboard. --select keeps the agent context small.

recipes.title: Export a course to markdown
recipes.explanation: Walks modules → lessons → attachments. One folder per course, ready for LLM ingestion.

recipes.title: Calendar to .ics
recipes.explanation: iCalendar 2.0. Drop into Google Calendar / Outlook / Apple Calendar.

recipes.title: Cross-entity SQL on synced data
recipes.command: skool-pp-cli sql 'SELECT post_id, COUNT(*) c FROM comments GROUP BY post_id ORDER BY c DESC LIMIT 5'
recipes.explanation: Read-only SELECT against the local SQLite. Run `skool-pp-cli sync` first to populate.

trigger_phrases: export this skool course to markdown
